cards.back: A data structure consisting of a sequence of nodes, where each node points to the next node in the sequence
A linear collection of data elements whose order is not given by their physical placement in memory
A linked list with a fixed length, where each node represents a single element, and the last node points to a null value
A structure with two pointers, `next` and `last`, to represent the last node of the chain
A data structure that follows the Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) principle, using a linked list to store elements
